title: Chapter 7 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of state management!

questions.question: What is state in a Flutter app?
options: Data that can change over time
questions.explanation: State is any data that can change and affect the UI.

questions.question: Ephemeral state is shared across multiple widgets.
questions.explanation: Ephemeral state is local to a single widget.

questions.question: To update a StatefulWidget, you call _____.
questions.explanation: setState triggers a rebuild.

questions.question: Why should you check 'mounted' before calling setState in an async callback?
options: To avoid calling setState after the widget is disposed
To ensure the widget is visible
questions.explanation: If the widget is no longer in the tree, calling setState causes an error.

questions.question: Lifting state up means moving state to a parent widget.
questions.explanation: State is moved to the nearest common ancestor of widgets that need it.

questions.question: Which widget allows efficient propagation of data down the tree without constructor parameters?
questions.explanation: InheritedWidget is designed for this purpose.

questions.question: Provider is built on top of _____.
questions.explanation: Provider uses InheritedWidget under the hood.

questions.question: ChangeNotifier requires you to call notifyListeners() after changing data.
questions.explanation: Otherwise, listeners won't be notified of the change.

questions.question: What does Consumer do in Provider?
options: It listens to a provider and rebuilds its child when the value changes
It provides a value to the widget tree
questions.explanation: Consumer rebuilds its builder when the listened object changes.

questions.question: To access a provider without rebuilding the widget, use context._____.
questions.explanation: context.read<T>() returns the value without listening.

questions.question: Selector rebuilds its builder whenever any part of the model changes.
questions.explanation: Selector rebuilds only when the selected value changes.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'selector' function in Selector?
options: To extract a part of the state to listen to
questions.explanation: The selector picks a specific value from the model to listen for changes.

questions.question: In Provider, to provide a ChangeNotifier, you use _____.
questions.explanation: ChangeNotifierProvider is specifically for ChangeNotifier subclasses.

questions.question: You can have multiple providers in the widget tree.
questions.explanation: Providers can be nested and combined using MultiProvider.

questions.question: Which method in ChangeNotifier notifies listeners?
questions.explanation: notifyListeners() triggers a rebuild of listeners.

questions.question: To pass a value that never changes, you might use a _____ provider.
questions.explanation: Provider is for providing a value without listening to changes.

questions.question: setState can be called from any method in a StatefulWidget.
questions.explanation: But only when the widget is mounted.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using Selector over Consumer?
options: It works with any data type
questions.explanation: Selector only rebuilds when specific parts of the state change, improving performance.

questions.question: The `child` parameter in Consumer is used to _____.
options: pass a child that doesn't depend on the provider
questions.correctAnswer: pass a child that doesn't depend on the provider
questions.explanation: The child is not rebuilt, which can optimize performance.

questions.question: InheritedWidget requires manual registration of dependents.
questions.explanation: Flutter automatically tracks dependents when you call dependOnInheritedWidgetOfExactType.

questions.question: Which package is NOT a state management solution?
questions.explanation: Dio is an HTTP client, not for state management.

questions.question: When lifting state up, you often pass callbacks to child widgets using _____.
questions.explanation: Callbacks are typically passed via constructor parameters.

questions.question: Provider automatically disposes ChangeNotifiers when the provider is removed from the tree.
questions.explanation: Yes, Provider handles disposal for you.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'create' parameter in ChangeNotifierProvider?
questions.explanation: create is a function that returns a new ChangeNotifier instance.

questions.question: To listen to a provider and rebuild when the value changes, use context._____.
questions.explanation: context.watch<T>() triggers a rebuild when the value changes.
